# opt crashes in llvm/lib/IR/DebugInfo.cpp:544

**Author:** Emilyaxe
**URL:** https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/173128
**Status:** Open
**Labels:** debuginfo, crash, generated by fuzzer

## Body

When running opt on the following IR, opt crashes. 
I am not sure whether this is a valid case. If using opt in this way is not appropriate, I am open to closing this issue.
godbolt: https://godbolt.org/z/oMjoTPEMz

llvm version 50ae726bb3498

test.ll

```
define i32 @negativity_1() {
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %0
  %phi = phi i32 [ 0, %0 ], [ %and2, %loop ]
  %and2 = and i32 %phi, -1
  %and3 = and i32 %and2, -32768
  %cond = icmp eq i32 %and3, 0
  br i1 %cond, label %exit, label %loop,!llvm.loop!1

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret i32 %and3
}

!0 =!{}
!1 = distinct!{}

```


commads
./bin/opt test.ll  -S


stacktrace

```
opt: /root/llvm-project/llvm/lib/IR/DebugInfo.cpp:544: llvm::MDNode* stripDebugLocFromLoopID(llvm::MDNode*): Assertion `!N->operands().empty() && "Missing self reference?"' failed.
PLEASE submit a bug report to https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/ and include the crash backtrace and instructions to reproduce the bug.
Stack dump:
```

---

### Comment 2 - OCHyams

I'm not overly familiar with loop metadata but according to https://llvm.org/docs/LangRef.html#llvm-loop (and that assert) it looks like loop metadata is expected to have at least a self reference operand, which would indicate this could be invalid IR. What or how was this IR generated?

---

### Comment 3 - Emilyaxe

> I'm not overly familiar with loop metadata but according to https://llvm.org/docs/LangRef.html#llvm-loop (and that assert) it looks like loop metadata is expected to have at least a self reference operand, which would indicate this could be invalid IR. What or how was this IR generated?

Hi, this IR was generated by fuzzer

---

### Comment 4 - OCHyams

In that case it's probably less urgent as it's not going to get hit with "normal" usage, but probably still worth keeping the ticket open and fixing if someone can get to it (I wonder if llvm-reduce ever reduces metadata down to this state and crashes?).  It would be ideal if opt handled this invalid input gracefully rather than crashing, though I don't have a good gut instinct as to how much work that would be or how similar invalid cases are handled.

---
